summary: feat(core): align providers with Responses APIs
decisions: Rename the model provider identity from openai-compatible to openai so configuration matches the concrete OpenAI Responses API adapter.
Route Grok search through /responses with the web_search tool, stream=false, configured model, status handling, and citation-based result mapping.
Update examples, policy defaults, product docs, and tests to match the provider keys and response contracts.

Session decision: Use one GitHub Actions CI workflow with official checkout/upload-artifact actions and rustup-managed stable Rust because it reduces supply-chain surface while keeping fmt, clippy, test, release build, and artifact upload reviewable.

Session decision: Implement M3 with a provider-neutral model contract, ModelProvider/ModelService, and a private OpenAI-compatible adapter over NetworkClient to avoid brittle MCP/orchestrator contracts.
Session decision: Implement M4 as an internal, MCP-free aspect_research runtime with budget/tool guards and output validation while deferring real provider tool-result round-tripping.
Session decision: Convert MoeResearch into a virtual Cargo workspace with moe-research-core, moe-research-cli, and moe-research-tests to establish runtime/CLI/test boundaries.
tests: Updated config/model/search tests for OpenAI provider naming and Grok Responses web_search request/response mapping.
risk: Grok search result extraction depends on Responses url_citation annotations; real API behavior should remain verified with live provider tests.

summary: feat(search): include grok search_sources as fallback evidence
decisions: Extend GrokSearchOutput::Message with a search_sources field so the adapter sees every URL Grok consulted, not only the ones inlined as url_citation annotations.
Append search_sources after citation-derived results in map_grok_response, deduped by URL and capped by max_results, so high-value references (reddit, substack) are no longer silently dropped.
Inherit the message-level full_text into the fallback Evidence summary and use the source title as the per-row snippet — the cleanest signal we have when no annotation indices are available.

summary: feat(net): trace-level wire body capture in reqwest_client
decisions: Keep all wire events under the existing moe_research_core::net::reqwest_client tracing target instead of opening a new sub-target — operators only need to learn one filter and trace level alone separates body capture from metadata.
Mint a fresh UUIDv4 correlation_id per send_once and stamp both outbound and inbound events with it; each retry attempt mints its own id so logical round trips never collide.
Gate body rendering behind tracing::enabled!(TRACE) inside helper functions so a debug-level run does not pay the cost of body to_string / truncation work.
Cap rendered bodies at 64 KiB with a JSON __truncated marker carrying original_bytes plus a UTF-8 safe head prefix — downstream log consumers stay parseable even on oversized responses.
Shrink the existing non-2xx debug body field to a 256-byte body_excerpt and steer operators toward reqwest_client=trace for the full payload, removing the double-log of large error bodies.
Hand-roll a ~40 line tokio TcpListener mock instead of pulling in wiremock, since the existing test crate already depends on tokio and reqwest.
tests: 8 inline unit tests for render_body_for_trace and excerpt_for_debug (UTF-8 boundary, exact cap, oversized, zero cap)

End-to-end MCP smoke test against real OpenAI + Grok providers: 5 outbound/inbound pairs captured with 5 unique correlation_ids, attempt=0 across all, body_truncated=false on all (max 22 KiB observed)

summary: docs(prompts): tighten verbatim evidence rule for aspect-agent
decisions: Replace the existing single-line `do not alter` clause with an explicit verbatim-copy rule that enumerates every prohibited transformation (paraphrasing, summarising, shortening, reformatting, translation, normalisation) so models stop hitting the validator's byte-equal provenance check after a long live run.
Spell out the validator behavior (rejects the entire output if any character differs) inside the prompt so the model can predict the failure mode without reading Rust code.
Tell the model to prefer omitting low-quality provenance fields or re-querying over rewriting them, so the verbatim rule cannot be bypassed by relabelling a paraphrase as `quality improvement`.

summary: fix(orchestrator): name offending fields in provenance error
decisions: Replace provenance_matches (bool) with provenance_mismatch_fields (returns Vec<&'static str>) so the validator can surface every diverging field in one issue instead of bisecting them across re-runs.

Lift the SchemaValidationFailed message from just the error code to code + path + first issue message; without the path an operator could not tell which evidence row (or which field on it) tripped the byte-equal check.
Build the issue path as evidence[N].<field> using the first mismatched field so logs jump straight to the diverging value in the wire trace.

bugs.symptom: On a long live aspect_research run the only failure surface was 'final output failed validation: mutated_evidence_provenance' with no field name and no index, so operators had to scan the entire wire trace to find which evidence row diverged.
bugs.root_cause: The validator collapsed all per-field comparison results into a single bool and used a path of evidence[N] with no field suffix, then the top-level error formatted only issues[0].code — three layers of lossy summarisation in a row.
bugs.fix: Return Vec<&'static str> of mismatched field names, embed them in the message, and append the first field to the path.

summary: feat(search): per-source local snippet and summary for grok citations
decisions: Stop assigning the entire output_text `full_text` to every citation-derived SearchResult.summary; build a per-citation excerpt instead, so two evidence rows from the same Grok response no longer carry one identical 1 KiB Markdown blob and consume Layer 2's prompt budget twice.

Introduce ExpandStrategy {Tight, Wide} so snippet and summary can share helpers but differ in boundary policy. Tight stops at the first whitespace just outside the citation range, Wide extends to the farthest whitespace within the byte budget, and Wide short-circuits to text edges (which outrank any interior whitespace).

Calibrate byte budgets via constants (SNIPPET_PAD_BYTES=80 / MAX=240, SUMMARY_PAD_BYTES=240 / MAX=600) instead of magic numbers; the snippet just needs a readable sentence, the summary is the model's reading material but still needs a cap.
Always trim and clamp to UTF-8 char boundaries and append an ellipsis when truncation occurs; fall back to a clamped excerpt of the whole text when annotation indices are missing or malformed.

Change ExecutionPolicy.timeout_ms from Option<u64> to DurationLimitMs (= Limit<u64>) so the field accepts the same `null` / `-1` / non-negative-integer encoding that AgentBudget.timeout_ms and BudgetConfig already use. Two encodings for the same concept (`Option<u64>` vs `Limit<u64>`) is the bug — callers should not have to know which mode each sibling field uses.
Add `#[serde(default = "DurationLimitMs::unlimited")]` so omitting the field deserialises as Unlimited, matching the prior Option<u64>::None semantics.

In validate_aspect_task, treat Unlimited as `inherit parent budget` (not `override with infinity`), so an unset execution policy cannot silently widen a finite parent budget while still letting callers pin a finite ceiling tighter than the parent.

bugs.symptom: Smoke-test caller wanting `no global ceiling, use per-aspect budget` could not encode it as -1 because the schema rejected it: `failed to deserialize parameters: invalid value: integer -1, expected u64`.
bugs.root_cause: ExecutionPolicy.timeout_ms was Option<u64>; only None encoded `unlimited`, while every sibling Limit<u64> field already accepted -1 or null. Two encodings for the same concept.
bugs.fix: Promote the field to DurationLimitMs and route every call site (agent_loop, research validation, test helpers) through the Limit pattern.

tests: Updated all `fn execution_policy(timeout_ms: Option<u64>)` helpers to map Option through Limit::limited / Limit::unlimited so existing call sites (`Some(N)` / `None`) keep working.
Updated direct field assignments in orchestrator_tests (`request.execution_policy.timeout_ms = Some(1)`) to `Limit::limited(N)`.

summary: feat(observability): log full validator error message on agent failure
decisions: Emit the internal Error Display (which includes the validator's code + path + message) on the two failure-path warn! sites (agent_loop and mcp/tools), so SchemaValidationFailed and BudgetExceeded events carry the actual diagnosis instead of just the public error code.

Keep the redacted code + public message as the only fields that leak into the MCP envelope; the new error_detail field lives only on stderr where it is visible to the operator but never to the MCP client.
Use the effective research budget at workflow entry points instead of an unlimited guard.
Merge operator config and Layer 1 request budgets by taking the stricter per-dimension limit.
Keep MCP schemas unchanged and cover the behavior with workflow and envelope regression tests.

bugs.symptom: aspect_research ignored configured research-level caps and deep_research did not merge config and request budgets.
bugs.root_cause: workflow.rs constructed research guards from unlimited or request-only budgets.
bugs.fix: derive effective budgets before execution and pass the shared guard through aspect runtimes.

summary: refactor(net): redact log output at the type boundary
decisions: Redact secrets inside custom Debug impls on the network DTOs so any log/format site is safe by construction, instead of relying on a RedactionPolicy invoked at chosen call sites.
Introduce log_safe wrapper types (SafeText, SafeUrl, SafeHeaderValue, SafeJson, SafeWireBody) as the single redaction boundary and delete policy.rs.
Log Error::public_message() on operator-facing MCP and agent-runtime events to avoid leaking internal validator paths and field names.
Performed a breaking product rename from Lapis to MoeResearch with no legacy CLI, config, or MCP fallback.
Renamed Cargo packages/directories to moe-research-* and Rust crates to moe_research_* to keep internal boundaries aligned with product identity.
Updated PM DeepResearch public docs, skills, and prompt assets from PR #20 to use MoeResearch MCP naming and mcp__moeresearch__ tool prefixes.
Kept cargo-dist generated WiX naming aligned with dist plan output while renaming the executable to moeresearch.

summary: docs: add project AI context documentation
decisions: Add a concise root CLAUDE.md plus detailed crate-level CLAUDE.md files so future AI/code agents can navigate MoeResearch without repeatedly rediscovering workspace architecture.
Document each domain crate at its ownership boundary instead of centralizing every detail in the root file; this keeps the root overview small while preserving module-specific context near the code.
Record module responsibilities, entry points, dependencies, testing guidance, and AI usage notes without changing source code.
tests: Documentation-only change; no runtime tests run.
risk: Low: generated documentation only; risk is stale or incomplete architectural notes rather than runtime behavior.

summary: ci(release): publish npm packages with token
decisions: Use the configured npm token for release publishing instead of GitHub OIDC provenance while npm scope/token access is being stabilized.
Remove id-token permission from the npm publish job because provenance signing is no longer requested.
tests: Ran ccg verify-security on .github/workflows/release.yml and dist-workspace.toml; no Critical or High findings.

summary: fix(mcp): clarify direct request payload contract
decisions: Strengthen MCP tool descriptions so direct Claude Code tool calls pass AspectResearchRequest or DeepResearchRequest directly rather than an outer JSON-RPC tools/call wrapper.
Keep the public MCP surface unchanged with only aspect_research and deep_research, and keep provider secrets, base URLs, and provider-native DTOs behind Rust configuration.
Document the direct-vs-raw MCP distinction in skills, Layer 1 prompts, and the MCP usage guide while preserving raw JSON-RPC examples for external clients.
Add direct payload fixtures and prompt skeleton checks to prevent schema drift in SearchPolicy fields, timeout relationships, and AspectResearchRequest retry shape.
Deflake the CLI serve stderr test by waiting for the startup log on the stderr pipe instead of sleeping a fixed interval before killing the process.

bugs.symptom: The full mcp_tests suite intermittently failed because the serve process could be killed before cargo run emitted the startup log on stderr.
bugs.root_cause: The test used a fixed five-second sleep before killing the child process, which was too short when startup included compile or scheduling latency.
bugs.fix: Read stderr concurrently, wait up to 30 seconds for the moeresearch initialized marker, then terminate and assert stdout remains protocol-clean.

risk: Low. Changes are metadata, documentation, prompt fixtures, and regression tests; no provider runtime behavior or public MCP tools were added.

summary: fix(cli): place MCP env flags after server name
decisions: Move the MCP server name before Claude Code environment flags so the variadic -e/--env option cannot consume the server name as an env value.
Keep env forwarding centralized in claude_mcp_add_argv so mcp register and onboard dry-run inherit the same parser-safe argument order.
Use the documented -e KEY=value spelling in generated commands while preserving redaction for dry-run command output and mcpServers JSON.
Lock the regression with CLI onboarding tests that validate both formatted dry-run output and fake Claude argv ordering.

bugs.symptom: moeresearch mcp register failed with Claude Code 2.1.181 when enabled provider env vars were present, reporting an invalid environment variable format for the server name.
bugs.root_cause: The generated claude mcp add argv placed --env KEY=value before the positional MCP server name; Claude Code parses --env as variadic and consumed the name as another env value.
bugs.fix: Emit the server name before repeated -e KEY=value flags, then emit -- before the moeresearch serve command.

risk: Low. Change is limited to CLI argv ordering, tests, and documentation; provider runtime and config schema are unchanged.
